name: Ad Lib Hotel Bangkok
features: World Architecture Festival award winner (2 category wins), rooftop saltwater pool, 24hr room service with full menu, free tuk-tuk shuttle to BTS Nana & Ploenchit, iconic banyan tree courtyard entrance, sustainable/eco-friendly operations (regional products, energy efficient rooms), pet-friendly (THB 1000/night + THB 2000 deposit), near Bumrungrad International Hospital, on-site Thai & international restaurant (24hr), hypoallergenic pillow menu, polished concrete & warm earthy design aesthetic, 68 rooms (25-89 sqm range), opened 2013, sister property Ad Lib Khon Kaen, represented by Heavens Portfolio in China/Korea/Singapore, Instagram @adlibbangkok (~7300 followers)
competitors: The Continent Hotel Bangkok, Muse Bangkok, Hua Chang Heritage Hotel, Hansar Bangkok, Ariyasom Villa, Cabochon Hotel, The Salil Hotel Sukhumvit, 137 Pillars Suites Bangkok, Sindhorn Midtown, COMO Metropolitan Bangkok, Kimpton Maa-Lai Bangkok, Oriental Residence Bangkok, The Siam, Sofitel Bangkok Sukhumvit, Hotel Nikko Bangkok
brief: Ad Lib Hotel Bangkok is a 68-room boutique design hotel in Sukhumvit Soi 1, Bangkok. Opened in 2013, it has won two World Architecture Festival awards. The hotel is known for its distinctive polished concrete and warm earthy design aesthetic, anchored by an iconic banyan tree courtyard at its entrance. Key amenities include a rooftop saltwater pool, 24-hour room service with a full Thai and international menu, free tuk-tuk shuttle to BTS Nana and Ploenchit stations, a fitness center, and pet-friendly policies. The hotel operates sustainably with regionally sourced products and energy-efficient rooms. Rooms range from 25 sqm (Plush) to 89 sqm (Sublime Plus with private jacuzzi and BBQ area), with hypoallergenic pillow menus and premium bed linens. It sits adjacent to Bumrungrad International Hospital, one of Asia's top medical tourism destinations. Room rates range from $80-200/night, positioning it in the mid-range to upscale boutique segment. The hotel has a sister property, Ad Lib Khon Kaen, and is represented internationally by Heavens Portfolio in the China, South Korea, and Singapore markets. On TripAdvisor and Booking.com, the hotel receives consistently high marks for breakfast quality, staff friendliness, design/ambiance, and the tuk-tuk shuttle service. Common criticisms include: some rooms lack natural light, basic rooms are small (25 sqm), and the hotel can be hard to find due to its hidden alley entrance.
